questions.question: What is the purpose of logistic regression?
keyPoints: Logistic regression is used for binary classification problems
It predicts the probability of an instance belonging to a particular class

questions.question: What is the sigmoid function?
keyPoints: The sigmoid function maps the input to a probability between 0 and 1

questions.question: What is the log loss function?
keyPoints: The log loss function measures the difference between the predicted probabilities and the actual labels
It is used to evaluate the performance of a logistic regression model

questions.question: What is regularization in logistic regression?
keyPoints: Regularization is used to prevent overfitting
It adds a penalty term to the cost function

questions.question: What is the difference between L1 and L2 regularization?
keyPoints: L1 regularization uses the absolute value of the coefficients
L2 regularization uses the square of the coefficients

questions.question: Explain the concept of logistic regression and its applications
keyPoints: Logistic regression is used for binary classification problems
It predicts the probability of an instance belonging to a particular class
It has applications in medical diagnosis, credit risk assessment, and marketing

questions.question: Explain the concept of the sigmoid function and its role in logistic regression
keyPoints: The sigmoid function maps the input to a probability between 0 and 1
It is used to model the probability of an instance belonging to a particular class

questions.question: Explain the concept of regularization in logistic regression and its importance
keyPoints: Regularization is used to prevent overfitting
It adds a penalty term to the cost function
It is important to prevent overfitting and improve the generalizability of the model
